title: chrysobalanus icaco var. chariensis
intro: Chrysobalanus icaco var. chariensis, also known as the jabillo or Caribbean copaiba tree, is a plant species within the family Chrysobalanaceae. The medicinal properties of Chrysobalanus icaco var. chariensis are attributed to its essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In terms of herbal preparations, Chrysobalanus icaco var. chariensis can be used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
